description: Corpus for recall evaluation — ~50 diverse memories across all kinds, wings, and rooms. Seeds into a fresh instance then runs recall_v2 queries against it.

memories.content: RLS is enforced at the database level on all tenant-scoped tables. The application role has NOBYPASSRLS. Application-level WHERE clauses are defense-in-depth, not a substitute for Row Level Security.
To deploy Engram: run docker compose up -d, then execute engram bootstrap-key to get the first API key. The service is reachable at http://localhost:8000.

Agent-sourced manual writes default to review_status='proposed' at 0.6 source_trust, preventing agents from getting user-level trust automatically. memory_confidence starts low and can be raised by usefulness signals or human review.

Auto-promotion Path A: a proposed item promotes to active if memory_confidence >= 0.7, no conflicts, 72h unchallenged, no dispute events. Path B requires 2+ distinct non-author principals marking it useful via feedback.

The embedding pipeline uses OpenRouter with encoding_format='float' because the OpenAI SDK defaults to base64 which OpenRouter does not support. The embedding_provider config must include the correct base_url.
Content is append-first: never UPDATE memory item content. Metadata changes go through PATCH which writes to item_events first, then updates the column. This is a core architectural constraint.
User prefers dark mode and never wants email notifications. User prefers concise responses without excessive hedging.
Dedup is enforced by a unique index on (tenant_id, workspace_id, principal_id, content_hash) WHERE valid_to IS NULL AND review_status != 'rejected'. content_hash is SHA-256 of canonicalized content.

Multi-tenant isolation: every table has tenant_id. workspace_members controls agent group membership with roles (owner/admin/member/viewer). RLS policies ensure queries only return rows for the current tenant.

The recall scoring formula is: score = (importance * 0.30) + (source_trust * 0.25) + (memory_confidence * 0.20) + (recency_bonus * 0.15) + (human_verified_bonus * 0.10). Pinned items bypass the formula.
We chose PostgreSQL with pgvector over Redis because RLS, ACID transactions, and vector search in one database eliminated the need for a separate vector store.

Startup recall is deterministic given state: same corpus, item states, and config produce the same output. It is NOT deterministic across time because recall mutates last_recalled_at and recall_count.
The Monomyth production database is at 10.0.0.40 port 3306, user eqemu, database name peq. Docker commands require sudo on monomyth-prod.
Monomyth EQEmu Lua recurring bug: zone global is nil in NPC events — use eq.get_zone_instance_id(). Perl to Lua conversions have wrong-arity eq.*() calls.
When hardening SSH on a multi-user host, restrict changes to the specific user via a Match User block, not the global sshd_config section. A global PasswordAuthentication no locks out all users.
rproxy at 10.0.0.3 handles nginx TLS for *.zutfen.com. Admin endpoints are restricted to 10.0.0.0/24 and 100.0.0.0/8 via nginx location blocks.
User delegates full authority — wants concrete verdicts, not option menus. Prefers root-cause code analysis over passive monitoring.
Building Zutfen LLC as AI-first company. Three-lane model: autonomous, draft-and-queue, decision-only. Monomyth is dogfooding ground before revenue products.
User prefers low-downtime migrations and consolidating idle power-hungry hosts to save electricity.
CUDA hardware validation is test-only. Pull branch, run tests, report. Do NOT fix code — branch author pushes fixes, validator pulls and retests.
Verify GitHub runner counts via the GitHub API, never infer from SSH reachability. User has runners not in the SSH config.

Engram is a standalone, multi-tenant institutional-memory service for AI agents. Its differentiation is not simply vector search. It is memory that can be trusted: explicit provenance, stable authority, review states, temporal validity.
The trust model has memory lifecycle states: observed, proposed, active, recalled, confirmed, stale, superseded, invalidated, archived. Only active items enter deterministic startup recall.

Conflict detection on POST /v1/remember: semantic similarity check against active items. If cosine > 0.85, classifier determines duplicate, refine, or contradict. Conditional supersession based on authority hierarchy.
We decided to use FastAPI over Flask because async support and Pydantic v2 validation were critical for the trust model's request validation needs.

To upgrade the dogfood database: back up first with pg_dump, start a temporary pgvector container on the data volume, apply migrations in sequence via psql, verify table counts and RLS, then baseline the migration tracker.
The API key format is eng_<key_id>_<secret>. The key_id is looked up by a unique partial index for O(1) verification. The secret is verified against a sha256 digest — no bcrypt, no full-table scan.
Cloudflare intercepts requests to engram.zutfen.com. Use curl --resolve or LAN IP for direct testing. The SDK works because it sends a proper User-Agent.
Never store API keys, passwords, or credentials in memory items. The safety module checks before every write and blocks with HTTP 422.
We must never bypass Row Level Security. RLS is foundational to multi-tenant isolation. A forgotten WHERE clause cannot cause a cross-tenant leak because the database enforces it.

The knowledge graph stores triples (subject, predicate, object) with temporal validity. KG triples inherit visibility from their source memory item. KG queries JOIN to memory_items and check visibility against the caller's scope.
Tunnels are cross-wing links connecting related memories across domains. A tunnel from wing_code/chromadb-setup to wing_myproject/planning lets recall follow threads across project boundaries.

Memory kinds are tenant-governed. The built-in kinds are fact, preference, doctrine, decision, invariant, observation, diary_entry, procedure, and summary. Each kind has behavior flags: singleton, requires_review, stays_in_recall_when_disputed.
Classification uses a two-layer approach: rules first (deterministic, instant), then optional LLM enrichment via async job. The write path never blocks on LLM classification.
The engram_memory Hermes plugin wraps engram-hooks as a MemoryProvider. It implements prepare_memory_write to intercept durable writes and reject trivial content before it reaches the service.

Self-service agent endpoints let users create agent principals and API keys within their own tenant without admin scope. POST /v1/agents creates principal plus key, GET /v1/agents lists them, DELETE revokes all keys.
All PRs must pass CI before merge. No exceptions, even for trivial docs changes.
Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, refactor:. One branch, one PR per logical change. Squash-merge.

The post-remediation audit found two high-severity concurrency holes in trust-state writers. Both AUTO_SUPERSEDE and manual invalidation were unlocked, unguarded writers of valid_to and superseded_by. Both are now closed with canonical pair locks.
The recall candidate selection uses a bounded SQL query with a ceiling to prevent unbounded corpus scans. Relationship-aware recall expands from semantic hits via memory_edges and tunnels.

Feedback events drive the quorum promotion path. Two or more distinct non-author principals marking an item useful promotes it regardless of age. Only user/admin feedback resets the startup recall penalty.
User values external design review before implementation. Seeks multi-LLM feedback on architecture docs, expects synthesis with adopt/defer/push-back verdicts.
Building Engram as open-source shared structured memory for multi-agent AI teams. Successor to MemPalace and CCA. Potential hosted service after self-hosted product is proven.
The LARQL CI cache uses sccache v0.16.0 with MinIO S3 on cibox3 at 10.0.0.33:9000. Four runners: cibox-proxmox, monomyth-build, cibox2, cibox3.
Frigate NVR runs in a Docker-in-LXC container on Proxmox. GPU passthrough required for object detection acceleration.
OpenWrt firewall rules: forward rules for port forwarding need both tcp and udp entries. NAT reflection enables hairpin access to public IPs from inside the LAN.

The session summary from the trust-state audit: we closed all five residual trust-state writer serialization gaps, delivered the canonical trust-proof selector, upgraded the dogfood database from migration level 001+002 to 013 with zero data loss, and recorded focused smoke evidence.
SQLite tests use comma-separated TEXT for scopes because SQLite cannot bind Python lists to array columns. The auth._parse_scopes function handles both TEXT and TEXT[] formats for this reason.
The guard pre-filter rejects content with fewer than 12 non-whitespace characters. It does not evaluate semantic meaning — semantic classification is the LLM layer.
The hosted product will bundle embeddings invisibly. Self-hosted users bring their own provider and key. There is no managed proxy tier — it is an open source project, not a charity.
